title: GitHub Gists
content:
### What are Gists?

GitHub Gists are simple, shareable code snippets. They can be single files or multi-file mini-repositories.

### Creating Gists

- **Public gists** — Visible to anyone, searchable, appear in your profile
- **Secret gists** — Not searchable, but accessible by anyone with the URL

Create via [gist.github.com](https://gist.github.com) or using `gh`:

```bash
gh gist create file.js --public
git gist create file.js --secret
```

### Embedding Gists

Paste the embed URL (`<script src="https://gist.github.com/user/gist_id.js"></script>`) into any HTML page. Gists automatically show syntax-highlighted code.

### Cloning Gists

```bash
git clone https://gist.github.com/username/gist_id.git
```

Gists are full Git repositories — you can commit, push, and pull changes.

### Gist Forks

Users can fork public gists to create their own copies. The fork chain is tracked, and original authors see notifications when their gist is forked.

quiz.question: What is the difference between a public gist and a secret gist?
options: Secret gists can include binary files

quiz.question: Can gists be cloned as Git repositories?
options: Yes, gists are full Git repos
Only secret gists can be cloned
Only public gists can be cloned
